revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Amayukawata-jinja]][[ja:天湯川田神社]] {{Expand Japanese|天湯川田神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Amayukawata Shrine | native_name = 天湯川田神社 | image = Amayukawata-jinja haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amayukawata Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.573644|135.632964}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|fr|Ama-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|it|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|mni|ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯀꯣꯌꯅꯦ|pt|Ame-no-Koyane|ru|Амэ-но коянэ-но микото|simple|Ame-no-Koyane|zh|天兒屋命|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} }} {{nihongo|'''Amayukawata Shrine'''|天湯川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần {{ill|Q11455616|ltq=Q11455616|qid=Q11455616|1=Sukunakawata Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Kashiwara|lt=Kashiwara|ar|كاشيوارا (أوساكا)|ca|Kashiwara|ceb|Kashiwara-shi|ce|Касивара|de|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|eo|Kaŝiŭara|es|Kashiwara (Osaka)|et|Kashiwara|fa|کاشی‌وارا، اوساکا|fi|Kashiwara|fr|Kashiwara|gl|Kashiwara|hy|Կաշիվարա|it|Kashiwara|ja|柏原市|ko|가시와라시|mg|Kashihara|mzn|کاشی‌وارا، اوساکا|nl|Kashiwara|pl|Kashiwara|pt|Kashiwara|ro|Kashiwara|ru|Касивара|sv|Kashiwara|tg|Кашивара|th|คาชิวาระ|tl|Kashiwara|tr|Kashiwara|tt|Касивара|uk|Кашівара|vi|Kashiwara, Ōsaka|war|Kashiwara, Osaka|zh_classical|柏原市|zh_min_nan|Kasiwara-chhī|zh_yue|柏原|zh|柏原市|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} * {{ill|Kawachi Province|lt=Kawachi Province|be|Каваты (правінцыя)|ca|Província de Kawachi|ckb|پارێزگای کاواچی|cs|Provincie Kawači|de|Provinz Kawachi|en|Kawachi Province|es|Provincia de Kawachi|fa|ولایت کاواچی|fr|Province de Kawachi|gl|Provincia de Kawachi|hak|Kawachi-koet|id|Provinsi Kawachi|it|Kawachi (provincia)|ja|河内国|ko|가와치국|lt|Kavačio žemė|nl|Kawachi (provincie)|pt|Província de Kawachi|ru|Кавати (провинция)|simple|Kawachi Province|sk|Kawači (provincia)|sr|Провинција Кавачи|tr|Kawachi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Каваті|zh_min_nan|Kawati-kok|zh_yue|河內國|zh|河內國|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} * {{ill|Q11438107|ltq=Q11438107|qid=Q11438107|1=Ōgata district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.573644|135.632964}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Amayukawata-jinja haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|fr|Ama-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|it|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|mni|ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯀꯣꯌꯅꯦ|pt|Ame-no-Koyane|ru|Амэ-но коянэ-но микото|simple|Ame-no-Koyane|zh|天兒屋命|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Q455602|ltq=Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180891 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5122005002302 == Commons category (P373) == * Amayukawata-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府柏原市高井田89番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amayukawata-jinja}} [[JA:天湯川田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63891125}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Amayukawata Shrine | Image=[[File:Amayukawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara City|Q873583|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} 89 Takaida | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities={{ill|lt=Amayukawa Itaki|Q52319907|qid=Q52319907|lt=Amayukawa-geta no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Yukawata}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Founded=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|qid=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Other names= | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amayukawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Amayukawata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine grounds entrance}}]] '''Amayukawata Shrine''' (あまゆかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Takaida, {{ill|lt=Kashiwara City|Q873583|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is enshrined on the site of the pagoda ruins of {{ill|lt=Torisaka-dera Ruins|Q92453846|qid=Q92453846|lt=Torisaka-dera|1=Tosaka-dera}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Kawachi Six Temples|Q21652904|qid=Q21652904|1=Kawachi Six Temples}} visited by Emperors Shōmu and Kōken during the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. The shrine grounds are maintained as Kashiwara City Takaida Park. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Amayukawa Itaki|Q52319907|qid=Q52319907|lt=Amayukawa-geta no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Yukawata}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume no Muchi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Among these, Amayukawa-geta no Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the ancient Toritori no Muraji clan that ruled the surrounding area. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but Torisaka-dera, said to have stood on the shrine grounds, was the clan temple of the Toritori clan. In the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, there are entries for Torisaka Village and Toritori Village in {{ill|lt=Ōagata District|Q11438107|qid=Q11438107|1=Ōgata district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}, suggesting that together with the nearby {{ill|lt=Shukunagawa Shrine|Q11455616|qid=Q11455616|1=Sukunakawata Shrine}}, the Toritori no Muraji clan enshrined their ancestral deity here. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Amayukawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province” and ranked as a minor shrine. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} through the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, it was known by names such as “Kawata Daimyōjin,” “Ōmiya Daimyōjin,” and “Kasuga Shrine,” but in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was renamed to its present name and designated a village shrine. === Shrine grounds === The grounds are currently maintained as “Kashiwara City Takaida Park,” with the shrine buildings standing together at the top of a hill within the park. === Torisaka-dera Pagoda Ruins === Within the grounds are remains of the ancient temple {{ill|lt=Torisaka-dera Ruins|Q92453846|qid=Q92453846|lt=Torisaka-dera|1=Tosaka-dera}}. Excavations conducted in Shōwa 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|qid=Q3696|1=1961}}) uncovered the pagoda’s central foundation stone and rainwater drainage ditches. The central foundation stone is an underground-style foundation embedded deeper than the podium surface, made of granite measuring 1.2 m on each side and 63 cm thick. The pillar hole has a conical reliquary cavity with a diameter of 50.5 cm at the base and a depth of 15.5 cm. Observation of the soil layers revealed that the central pillar was wrapped in clay and then backfilled in alternating layers of charcoal and soil mixed with small stones. Based on the size of the pillar hole and the distance between the foundation stone and the drainage ditches, the pagoda is estimated to have been a three-story pagoda approximately 20 m tall, constructed in the mid to late 7th century. File:Amayukawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Amayukawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Amayukawata-jinja, tou.jpg|Memorial tower for the war dead === Access === *{{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|qid=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara City, Osaka)|Q856508|qid=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (5-minute walk) *{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line (Kintetsu)|Q1052545|qid=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Andō Station|Q4761545|qid=Q4761545|1=Andō Station}} (10-minute walk) === References === *Shrine notice board (Kashiwara City Board of Education, 1990) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Amayukawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Shukunagawa Shrine|Q11455616|qid=Q11455616|1=Sukunakawata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amayukawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amayukawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:堅下]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]]
